title: Egyptian civilization_History of Egypt, Chaldæa, Syria, Babylonia, and Assyria by G. Maspero
paragraphs: The Column of Taharqa, at Karnak, features a mural depicting a lion emerging from its cage. The Battle of Tulliz Khumb-nigash is also represented. A proclamation declares Thumman as king. The head of Thumman was sent to Nineveh. Two Elamite chiefs were flayed alive. In the desert, a prayer is offered after painting by Gerome. An illustrated manuscript in hieroglyphics exists. Decorations on the wrappings of a mummy can be found at the Chieck Beled—Gizeh Museum. People have contributed their time and resources to make this happen.
